pages.raw_text:
Does every woman in Spokane


believe that their sex should have


the right to vote at all elections?


If the crosses on The Press bal


lots are any criterion, this answer


would be answered emphatically


In the affirmative. OF a desk


drawerful of ballots received in the


contest, as it might be called, not


one ballot has been marked no"


But this simply means that the


supporters of equal suffrage are


rallying to it's cause, like the we'll
organized army they are. They


want to show Congressman Poin-
dexter and the whole august and
statesman-like bunch at Washing
ton that the women of Spokane


want and are going to get the
right to vote.


Now, of course, there are heaps
and heaps of women who think


that the men ought to do the vot-
ing, and the women ought to at
tend solely to the household. But
how are they going to make this
opinion continue to rule, if they


don't express their stand? Let's
hear from both sides On the big


question, and find out just what the


women of Spokane think. Either


bring or mail your ballots to the
suffrage editor. The Spokane Press.
Two prominent suffraget leaders


expressed themselves on the mat
ter today as follows:


Mrs. d. G.. Coates:


Women should have the ballot
because they have as much inter-
est as men in controlling the gov
ernment. Women are taxed as
heavily, and therefore should have
a voice and be an actor in real
life, instead of spectator"
Mrs. l.. NJ. House:


Government is made by the voice
